entries.intro: More new faces around here than I've seen in years. Not sure if that's good or bad.
details: Three families moved in last season alone. Came from somewhere east: didn't say much about why.
The market's busier, which is nice. But the inn's packed every night and the prices show it.
reactions: Change comes whether you invite it or not. Might as well get used to it.

statCheck.pass: People move toward trade and safety: always have. If this place is drawing folk, it means things are going well enough here.
statCheck.fail: I don't know why everyone's showing up now. Just feels different.

entries.intro: A family that's been here for generations packed up and left last week. Just like that.
details: They didn't make a fuss about it. Loaded up a cart one morning and were gone by noon.
Their neighbor said they had people somewhere south. Wanted to be closer to kin, apparently.
reactions: I understand it: family pulls you places. But it's strange seeing their house sit empty.

entries.intro: A young couple settled in recently and they've been nothing but helpful. Good neighbors.
details: They brought bread around to introduce themselves. When's the last time anyone did that?
The husband's already patching a fence for the old woman next door. Didn't even have to be asked.
reactions: It's refreshing. I hope they stick around.

entries.intro: Half the young folk around here have left for bigger settlements. Can't blame them, but it stings.
details: There's not much work here for someone with ambition. They go where the opportunities are.
My own nephew left two seasons ago. Sends letters sometimes: says he's doing well enough.
reactions: A place gets quiet when the young ones leave. You feel it in the evenings especially.

statCheck.pass: It happens in cycles: they leave when they're young and some come back when they're older and want the quiet. Just takes time.
statCheck.fail: I don't know if they'll come back. I hope so.

entries.intro: There's been a steady trickle of travelers passing through lately. More than usual.
details: Most of them don't stop long: a night or two, then they move on. Heading west, mostly.
A few asked about work, but there's not much to offer. They seemed decent enough.
reactions: Travelers come and go. It's only odd when you notice a pattern.
